title: What Is Ism al-Faʿil (the Active Participle)?

sections.text: Consider the following sentences:

items.text: — The man told the truth / The man is truthful.
— The wrongdoer regretted / The wrongdoer is regretful.
— The teacher struck the pupil / The teacher is one who strikes.

sections.text: If you reflect on the underlined words, you find them to be derived nouns on the pattern 'faʿil', indicating the one from whom the action proceeds — i.e., the doer. So 'sadiq' indicates the doer of truthfulness, 'nadim' indicates the doer of regret, and so on. For this reason each of these words is called: ism al-faʿil (the active participle).
Definition of Ism al-Faʿil
Ism al-faʿil (the active participle): a derived noun on the pattern 'faʿil', indicating the one who performs the action.
Forming Ism al-Faʿil
1 — Ism al-faʿil is formed from the triliteral verb on the pattern 'faʿil'.
Example:

items.text: (he drank / a drinker)
(he slept / a sleeper)
(he worked / a worker)

sections.text: 2 — Ism al-faʿil is formed from a non-triliteral verb by converting the verb to the present tense, replacing the present-tense letter with a damma-bearing mim, and giving kasra to the letter before the last.
Example:

items.text: (he received / a receiver)
(he became upright / one upright)
(he sinned / a sinner)

sections.text: Question: Could you explain in detail how to form ism al-faʿil from a non-triliteral verb?

Yes — let us apply the rule above:
(a) We have non-triliteral past verbs: istaqama, istaqbala, adhnaba.
(b) We convert them to the present tense: yastaqbilu, yastaqimu, yudhnibu.
(c) We replace the present-tense letter with a damma-bearing mim and put kasra before the last letter: mustaqbil, mustaqim, mudhnib.
Examples of Ism al-Faʿil

items.text: (a doer of good)
(a seller)
(a writer)
(a vengeful one)
(a wasteful one)
(a questioner)
(one who has cut off / disconnected)
(one who is lost)
(a player)

sections.text: Ism al-Faʿil and Its Operation
Ism al-faʿil that operates like its verb appears in two forms:

1 — Made Definite with 'Al-'
In this form, ism al-faʿil performs its operation absolutely, without any further condition. E.g.:

items.text: — The thief stole the money.

sections.text: This sentence consists of a verb (saraqa), a subject (al-liss), and a direct object (al-mal). Let us convert it to ism al-faʿil:

items.text: — This is the one who stole the money.

sections.text:
Ism al-faʿil: al-sariq.
al-mal: a direct object of ism al-faʿil.

items.text: — The enemy claimed that defeating him was impossible.

sections.text:
al-intisar: the first direct object of the verb 'zaʿama'.
mustahilan: the second direct object of the verb 'zaʿama'.
Let us convert it to ism al-faʿil:

items.transliteration: hadha al-ʿaduww-u al-zaʿim-u al-intisar-a ʿalayh-i mustahilan
items.text: — This is the enemy who claims that defeating him is impossible.

sections.text:
al-intisar: the first direct object of ism al-faʿil 'al-zaʿim'.
mustahilan: the second direct object of ism al-faʿil 'al-zaʿim'.

2 — Free of 'Al-'
In this form, ism al-faʿil operates like its verb only under two conditions:
(a) The meaning of the sentence — including ism al-faʿil — must be such that it pertains to the present or future, not the past. E.g.:

items.transliteration: al-muʿallim-u sharih-un al-dars-a al-an aw ghadan
items.text: — The teacher is explaining the lesson now or [will explain it] tomorrow.

sections.text: (b) Ism al-faʿil must depend on something preceding it — e.g., it falls after:
— An interrogative, e.g.:

items.text: — Is Muhammad reciting his poem?

sections.text: — A negation, e.g.:

items.text: — Your brother is not heeding my advice.

sections.text: — A vocative, e.g.:

items.text: — O you who stretch out your hands in supplication!

sections.text: — Falling as an attribute (sifa), e.g.:

items.text: — I saw a building reaching the clouds.

sections.text: — Falling as a predicate (khabar), e.g.:

items.text: — The truth is the refuter of falsehood.

sections.text: — Falling as a circumstantial accusative (hal), e.g.:

items.text: — I heard Muhammad reciting his poem.

sections.text: Iʿrab of Ism al-Faʿil

items.text: — The boy is truthful.

sections.text:
al-ghulam: mubtadaʾ in the nominative, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
sadiq: khabar in the nominative, marked by the manifest damma at its end.

items.text: — Allah Most High said: "Creator of the heavens and the earth, [who] made the angels messengers..." — Quran 35:1 (Sahih International)

sections.text:
fatir: a naʿt for the divine name in the genitive, marked by the manifest kasra; a mudaf.
al-samawat: a mudaf ilayh in the genitive, marked by the manifest kasra.
wa-l-ard: the waw is a conjunction; al-ard is conjoined upon al-samawat in the genitive, marked by the manifest kasra.
jaʿil: a second naʿt for the divine name; an ism al-faʿil annexed to its direct object (al-malaʾika); when annexed to al-malaʾika, it becomes transitive to the [second] direct object 'rusulan'; a mudaf.
al-malaʾika: a mudaf ilayh in the genitive, marked by the manifest kasra.
rusulan: a direct object of the annexed ism al-faʿil 'jaʿil', in the accusative, marked by the manifest fatha.

items.text: — Allah Most High said: "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham?" — Quran 19:46 (Sahih International)

sections.text:
a-raghib: the hamza is interrogative; raghib is a fronted khabar in the nominative, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
anta: a separate pronoun fixed on fatha, in the position of nominative as a postponed mubtadaʾ.
ʿan: a preposition.
aliha-ti: a noun in the genitive, marked by kasra, attached to 'raghib'; the yaʾ is a connected pronoun in the position of genitive as a mudaf ilayh.
ya: a vocative particle.
Ibrahim: a vocative fixed on damma, in the position of accusative.
Examples of Ism al-Faʿil from the Noble Quran
Allah Most High said:

items.text: — "Then would you possibly leave [out] some of what is revealed to you, or is your breast constrained by it..." — Quran 11:12 (Sahih International)
— "Indeed, I will make upon the earth a successive authority." — Quran 2:30 (Sahih International)
— "Our Lord, take us out of this city of oppressive people..." — Quran 4:75 (Sahih International)
— "I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me." — Quran 27:32 (Sahih International)
— "Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women..." — Quran 33:35 (Sahih International)

sections.text: Exercises on Ism al-Faʿil
1 — Identify every ism al-faʿil in the following passage and provide its full vowel-marking; indicate which is from a triliteral verb and which is from a non-triliteral verb:

— Light and darkness have a clear effect on a person's health. Whoever lives in a dark dwelling that the sun's rays do not fill is seen with a wilted body and a pale complexion.
The sun's light is beneficial in many ways: it dries the air, kills disease germs, and helps reduce humidity. So make sure it is present in your home so that you may live with a healthy body, full of strength and vigor. Beware of weak artificial light, for it spoils the air and tires the power of sight.
2 — Complete the following sentences by inserting an ism al-faʿil in the blank, and indicate its position in iʿrab:

items.text: — The vessel was ……
— Perhaps your father is ……
— The fire is …… in the house.
— I sailed the sea ……

sections.text: 3 — Provide the iʿrab for the underlined portions:

items.text: — Allah Most High said: "Indeed, I will make upon the earth a successive authority." — Quran 2:30 (Sahih International)
— [Classical poetry]: 'The slayer with the sword in the body of the slain — for swords, as for men, have appointed terms.'
